evals.prompt: I need to add a new 'notifications' feature to my NestJS backend. Users should be able to list their notifications, mark them as read, and delete them. The notifications are stored in a PostgreSQL database via Supabase. Design the full module structure — controller, service, DTOs, and module wiring. Show me the file layout and the key code for each file.
evals.expected_output: A complete feature module with controller (thin, 3 routes), service (business logic), DTOs (Zod or class-validator), module wiring, and proper layer separation. Should include proper guards, validation, and response formatting.

assertions.name: has_module_structure
assertions.description: Output includes a clear file/directory layout for the notifications feature

assertions.name: thin_controller
assertions.description: Controller delegates to service, no business logic in controller methods

assertions.name: has_dtos
assertions.description: Includes request/response DTOs with validation decorators or Zod schemas

assertions.name: has_module_wiring
assertions.description: Includes a NestJS @Module with providers, controllers, and imports

assertions.name: layer_separation
assertions.description: Service handles business logic, controller handles HTTP, no mixing

evals.prompt: My NestJS API has inconsistent error responses — some endpoints throw raw HttpExceptions, others return custom objects. I want to centralize error handling with a global exception filter and typed error definitions. The API should return errors in a consistent format with error codes. Show me how to set this up from scratch.
evals.expected_output: A BusinessException class, ERROR_DEFINITIONS constants, a GlobalExceptionFilter, and instructions for registering it as APP_FILTER in a CoreModule. Should explain the 'log or throw, never both' principle and show anti-patterns to avoid.

assertions.name: has_exception_class
assertions.description: Defines a custom exception class (e.g., BusinessException) with structured fields

assertions.name: has_error_definitions
assertions.description: Includes typed error definition constants with code, message, and httpStatus

assertions.name: has_global_filter
assertions.description: Implements an ExceptionFilter with @Catch() that formats all errors consistently

assertions.name: consistent_error_format
assertions.description: Error response includes statusCode, message, code, and timestamp fields

assertions.name: registration_shown
assertions.description: Shows how to register the filter as APP_FILTER in a module

evals.prompt: I'm building a NestJS API that needs three levels of access: public endpoints (health check, docs), authenticated user endpoints, and admin-only endpoints. How should I structure the guards and decorators? I'm using JWT for auth. Show me the guard implementations and how to apply them to different controllers.
evals.expected_output: A layered guard architecture with AuthGuard (JWT validation), RolesGuard (role checking), @Public() decorator, @Roles() decorator, global registration order, and usage examples on controllers.

assertions.name: has_auth_guard
assertions.description: Implements an AuthGuard that validates JWT Bearer tokens

assertions.name: has_roles_guard
assertions.description: Implements a RolesGuard that checks user roles against route requirements

assertions.name: has_public_decorator
assertions.description: Defines a @Public() decorator to skip authentication on specific routes

assertions.name: has_roles_decorator
assertions.description: Defines a @Roles() decorator to specify required roles

assertions.name: guard_registration
assertions.description: Shows global guard registration with APP_GUARD and explains execution order

assertions.name: usage_examples
assertions.description: Shows concrete controller examples for public, authenticated, and admin routes
